source_intake_record.raw_question_text:
Sonja's smartphone displays the diagram on the right. It shows how long she has worked with four different apps in the previous week. This week he has spent only half the amount of time using two of the apps and the same amount of time as last week using the other two apps. Which of the following pictures could be the diagram for the current week?
<image1>
<image2>
extraction_notes: options field contains only option labels without descriptive text

issues: Metadata extraction notes state that the options field contains only option labels without descriptive text.

image_dependency: question_text explicitly refers to 'the diagram on the right' and asks which picture could represent the current week
requires_image=true and text_dominant=false indicate the problem is primarily visual
asset_registry_record shows an image exists and the options field contains only labels, so the option content must be read from images
multi_step: solver must first extract last week's app-time relationships from the diagram
solver must apply the condition that two apps are halved while the other two stay the same
solver must compare the transformed pattern against multiple visual answer choices, combining visual reading and conditional reasoning
verifiability: answer_type is option/choice and answer_text='C', so the final response format is clear
registry_passed=true and the image asset exists, enabling downstream visual verification
verifiability is reduced somewhat because the choices have no descriptive text and correctness depends on interpreting image-only options

risk_flags: Correctness depends on accurate visual parsing of the base diagram and candidate pictures
Options are image-only; the choices field contains only labels without descriptive text

decision_reasons: Core assets are present: question text, primary image, and a valid choice answer are all available.
Registry passed, and the existing image supports this highly image-dependent problem.
Initial scores indicate strong educational value and recoverability despite the visual dependence.
Although the options field lacks descriptive text and correctness depends on image parsing, the necessary semantic content still appears recoverable from the provided image.
